output: Rewrote `useAutoSaveSettings` onto a per-domain `createEntityStore` adapter, preserved the public API, added stable custom-domain keying for generation edit settings, and hardened the factory bootstrap path so pending edits survive load/bootstrap without premature saves. Verified with the full hook/store modules exercised in this batch.

task_updates.executor_notes: Rewrote `useAutoSaveSettings` around a per-domain `createEntityStore` registry while preserving the existing return shape and semantics. The adapter now keeps callback/object references stable, supports `updateTextFields`, `initializeFrom`, and `saveImmediate(dataToSave?)`, uses a fixed custom-domain key for generation edit settings, and routes persistence through the existing `useToolSettings` / custom load-save backends. To preserve prior autosave behavior during loading and entity bootstrap, I also tightened `createEntityStore` so bootstrap is atomic, pending edits survive load resolution, loading-time updates do not persist prematurely, custom entities can be reloaded explicitly, and hook-driven load errors no longer leak as unhandled rejections. Verified with full module tests: `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/settings/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts`, `npx vitest run src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts`, and `npx vitest run src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useGenerationEditSettings.test.ts`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `useAutoSaveSettings` now fronts a per-domain `createEntityStore` registry instead of its bespoke reducer/debounce layer, while keeping the caller-facing API intact. The adapter preserves `updateTextFields`, `initializeFrom`, and `saveImmediate(dataToSave?)`, continues to use the existing `useToolSettings` and custom load/save persistence paths, adds a stable custom domain key for generation edit settings, and returns stable callbacks/object references when inputs are unchanged.
